title: 'StravaLeaks': How Le Monde located 18,000 French military personnel with a fitness app
subhed: For more than two years, Le Monde has been tracking a significant proportion of France's military personnel on Strava, a popular fitness app, on almost a daily basis. Their movements can be monitored, including in sensitive areas, putting both military operations and the servicemembers involved in them at risk.

Emmanuel Grégoire takes office as the new Paris mayor
subhed: On Sunday, the Socialist was officially elected by his peers on the Council of Paris, in which the political balance of power remains largely unchanged after the municipal elections. The left will govern for a fifth consecutive term.

title: Adrien Bilal, winner of the 2026 Best Young Economist Award: 'Climate change will cost 50% of GDP by 2100'
subhed: The winner of the 27th edition of the prize, created in 2000 by Le Monde and Le Cercle des Economistes, has developed a model that better captures the global impacts of climate disruption.

title: Is it ever a good idea to... wear leather pants?
subhed: In fashion, as in life, it is important to know the lines you should not cross. Or do so deliberately.

title: Flavel, a victim of police violence, tells his story: 'If there hadn't been videos, no one would ever have believed me'
subhed: On March 16, the former musician went out to order food in Noisiel, in the eastern Paris suburbs. Police violently arrested him, as seen in a video widely shared online. He described to Le Monde the events that led him to file a complaint with the internal affairs agency.

In wartime Tehran, rare photos capture daily life under bombardment
subhed: The photographs taken by an Iranian photographer and published by Le Monde offer a unique window into the capital's daily life. With 14 million inhabitants, Tehran has been shrouded in a media blackout since February 28. For his safety, the photographer has requested anonymity.

title: Revealed: Satellite image shows mysterious cargo at Iranian nuclear site in 2025
subhed: Le Monde reveals an image of a convoy at the gates of a major underground nuclear complex in Iran. According to several experts, it could be part of the country's stockpile of enriched uranium.

title: 'No Kings' protests against Trump focus on war in Iran: 'The US is on the wrong side of history'
subhed: Millions turned out to protests across the US against the Trump administration, with the movement's epicenter located in the twin cities of Minneapolis-Saint Paul, after two Americans were killed by federal agents there in January.
